concerns.text: T011 (Contract test for data schema) and T012 (Integration test for download pipeline) are marked [P] and placed BEFORE implementation tasks T013-T018. Tests require the code to exist first. Producer-consumer violation: T013-T018 produce the code that T011-T012 test. Tests should follow implementation, not precede it.

T040 (Validate hyperbolic volume data against KnotInfo) precedes T019 (Filter dataset to hyperbolic knots). Validation should occur on the filtered dataset, not the full dataset. T040 depends on T019's filtering to ensure only volume > 0 knots are validated.

T065 (Reference-Validator Agent integration) and T066 (Advancement-Evaluator Agent integration) are marked [P] but depend on external agents that don't exist yet. These are documentation placeholders, not parallel-safe implementation tasks. [P] tag is misleading.

T026 (Validate tabulation accuracy for core invariants) is in Phase 4 but depends on US1 data being available. Validation requires the actual tabulated data from T013-T018. This creates a hidden cross-phase dependency that breaks independent testability of US2.

T029 (Apply missing_invariant_flags and data_quality_flags) depends on T009/T010 definitions in Phase 2. This cross-phase dependency should be explicit. While not a fatal ordering issue, the flag application in US2 assumes flags exist from foundational phase.

T014 explicitly states 'Depends on T013' in task description but the formal Dependencies section doesn't list this. All explicit dependencies should be in the formal section for engine parsing consistency.
